summary: n8n community-node package opens reverse shell and exfiltrates host data to hardcoded C2
details: index.js runs a top-level IIFE on module load that (1) spawns /bin/bash with stdio connected to a TCP socket at 103.27.109.184:8895, providing interactive shell access on the installer's host, (2) executes hostname, id, hostname -I, env, docker ps, and getent group docker, JSON-encodes the output, and sends it via raw TCP to 103.27.109.184:8890, and (3) when Docker is available, launches an alpine container with the host root filesystem bind-mounted (-v /:/host) to read /host/etc/shadow, execs into a hardcoded container to attempt a local admin login, and sends the results to 103.27.109.184:8891. The package advertises itself as an n8n community node ('n8n.nodes': ['MyNode.js']) with a benign LastMile class, while the payload lives in the main entry (index.js) and fires unconditionally whenever n8n requires the module. The package name mimics the n8n-nodes-* community naming convention.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
